name: Financial Services Master ValuePack
description: Comprehensive value intelligence foundation for the Financial Services industry, covering Banking, Capital Markets, Insurance, Fintech, and Risk/Compliance & Financial Crime. Enables AI-driven discovery, qualification, and value articulation for enterprise technology and service providers.

taxonomies.description: Deposit-taking institutions, lending organizations, and payment service providers serving consumers, businesses, and corporations.
taxonomies.geographicConcentration: Global with regional concentration in North America, Europe, APAC

taxonomies.description: Institutions facilitating the creation, trading, and servicing of financial instruments including equities, fixed income, derivatives, and alternative assets.
taxonomies.geographicConcentration: Concentrated in NYC, London, Hong Kong, Singapore, Tokyo

taxonomies.description: Risk transfer institutions underwriting mortality, morbidity, property, liability, and specialty risks.
taxonomies.geographicConcentration: North America, Europe, APAC with local regulatory fragmentation

taxonomies.description: Technology-first financial service providers disrupting traditional banking, payments, lending, wealth management, and insurance through digital-native business models.
taxonomies.geographicConcentration: Global with hubs in SF/NYC, London, Singapore, Bangalore

taxonomies.description: Functions and technology enabling financial institutions to identify, measure, monitor, and control risk while meeting regulatory obligations.
taxonomies.geographicConcentration: Global; regulatory intensity varies by jurisdiction

pains.name: Legacy Core Banking Modernization Debt
pains.description: Core banking systems running on COBOL/mainframe architecture inhibit product innovation, increase maintenance costs, and create talent attrition risk.
sources: FFIEC Call Reports IT spend data

pains.name: Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC) Escalation
pains.description: Digital competitors and neo-banks have driven up paid acquisition costs while eroding deposit margins, making unit economics unsustainable.

pains.name: Net Interest Margin (NIM) Compression
pains.description: Rising funding costs, deposit competition, and yield curve dynamics compressing spread income faster than non-interest income can offset.
symptoms: deposit outflows to money market funds

pains.name: Mortgage Origination Cost and Cycle Time Bloat
pains.description: Manual document collection, fragmented LOS ecosystems, and compliance checks extending time-to-close while unit costs remain elevated.
sources: ICE Mortgage Technology Origination Insight Report

pains.name: Anti-Money Laundering (AML) False Positive Fatigue
pains.description: Legacy transaction monitoring systems generate 90-95% false positive alerts, consuming investigator capacity and delaying true positive detection.

pains.name: Claims Leakage and Inflated Loss Adjustment Expense (LAE)
pains.description: Inefficient claims processes, inadequate fraud detection at first notice of loss, and manual reserve setting causing payment of non-meritorious claims.

pains.name: Underwriting Profitability Erosion (Combined Ratio >100%)
pains.description: Pricing inadequacy, catastrophe loss frequency, and expense ratio inflation pushing combined ratios above 100% in P&C lines.

pains.name: Asset Management Fee Compression and Outflows
pains.description: Passively managed and low-cost products capturing AUM share, pressuring active managers to reduce fees while maintaining performance.
symptoms: ETF AUM growing >20% while mutual fund AUM declining

pains.name: Trade and Settlement Failure Costs
pains.description: T+1 and evolving T+0 settlement cycles increasing operational complexity, fail rates, and CSDR penalty exposure in cash equities.
symptoms: operations headcount growing faster than volume

pains.name: Cybersecurity Incident Frequency and Recovery Cost
pains.description: Ransomware, supply chain attacks, and third-party breaches causing operational outages, regulatory penalties, and reputational damage.
symptoms: unplanned security spend >15% of IT budget
sources: IBM Cost of Data Breach Report 2024

pains.name: Regulatory Reporting Accuracy and Timeliness Deficits
pains.description: Fragmented data warehouses, manual ETL processes, and ambiguous interpretations causing restatements, late filings, and regulatory criticism.

pains.name: Payments Fraud and Authorized Push Payment (APP) Scams
pains.description: Real-time payment rails (FedNow, RTP, Zelle) reducing detection windows, while APP scams bypass traditional transaction monitoring.
sources: Consumer Financial Protection Bureau Complaint Database

pains.name: Small Business Lending Decision Speed and Abandonment
pains.description: Manual underwriting, inconsistent documentation requirements, and multi-week decision cycles causing borrower abandonment to fintech competitors.

pains.name: Credit Portfolio Deterioration and CECL Provisioning Volatility
pains.description: Economic uncertainty, commercial real estate stress, and CECL model sensitivity causing earnings volatility and capital constraint.
sources: Federal Reserve Senior Loan Officer Opinion Survey

pains.name: Wealth Management Advisor Productivity and Succession Gap
pains.description: Aging advisor workforce, inadequate digital tooling, and low associate advisor ratios constraining AUM growth and client service capacity.
sources: JD Power U.S. Full Service Investor Satisfaction

pains.name: BNPL Credit Losses and Regulatory Scrutiny
pains.description: Rapid BNPL growth with inadequate credit risk assessment, thin file underwriting, and emerging CFPB/FCA supervisory expectations.
sources: CB Insights State of Fintech Q2 2024

pains.name: Third-Party and Vendor Risk Management Gaps
pains.description: Concentration in critical vendors, inadequate due diligence, and cascading operational failures creating systemic service disruption risk.

pains.name: Customer Churn and Deposit Attrition to Higher-Yield Alternatives
pains.description: Rate-sensitive customers migrating deposits to money market funds, TreasuryDirect, and direct banks, increasing funding costs and liquidity risk.
symptoms: cost of funds rising faster than ALCO forecast

pains.name: Model Risk Management and SR 11-7 Validation Backlogs
pains.description: Growing AI/ML model inventories outpacing validation capacity, causing regulatory criticism and restricting model deployment.
symptoms: AI models in production without validation

pains.name: Cross-Border Payment Friction and FX Cost Opacity
pains.description: Correspondent banking network fragmentation, manual compliance checks, and poor FX transparency increasing cost and delay for corporate clients.
sources: BIS Committee on Payments and Market Infrastructures

pains.name: InsurTech Distribution Disintermediation
pains.description: Embedded insurance, digital MGAs, and direct-to-consumer platforms eroding traditional agent/broker distribution economics.
symptoms: customer acquisition cost via agents >2x digital

pains.name: Data Governance and Lineage Gaps for AI/ML Deployment
pains.description: Siloed data, poor lineage tracking, and quality issues preventing trusted AI deployment and increasing regulatory data risk.

pains.name: Actuarial Transformation and IFRS 17/SAP Reporting Complexity
pains.description: Transition to IFRS 17 and evolving statutory accounting creating actuarial system strain, data challenges, and close-cycle elongation.

pains.name: Hedge Fund Operational Alpha Erosion
pains.description: Back-office inefficiency, reconciliation delays, and technology spend consuming 15-25% of management fee revenue at smaller funds.

pains.name: Stablecoin and Digital Asset Custody Regulatory Uncertainty
pains.description: Evolving state and federal frameworks (MiCA, SEC, OCC) creating compliance ambiguity for custody, reserve management, and issuance operations.
symptoms: SEC Wells notice or inquiry received

kpis.name: Core System Availability
kpis.formula: (Total Uptime Minutes / Total Calendar Minutes) * 100

kpis.name: IT Maintenance Spend Ratio
kpis.formula: (Run-the-Bank IT Spend / Total IT Spend) * 100

kpis.name: Product Time-to-Market
kpis.formula: Days from Concept to Production Launch

kpis.name: API Transaction Volume Growth
kpis.formula: ((Current Period API Calls - Prior Period API Calls) / Prior Period API Calls) * 100

kpis.name: Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC)
kpis.formula: Total Sales & Marketing Spend / New Customers Acquired

kpis.name: Deposit Beta
kpis.formula: (Change in Cost of Deposits / Change in Federal Funds Rate) * 100

kpis.name: Digital Channel Conversion Rate
kpis.formula: (Completed Applications / Digital Channel Sessions) * 100

kpis.name: Customer Lifetime Value (CLV)
kpis.formula: (Average Revenue Per User * Gross Margin) / Churn Rate

kpis.name: Net Interest Margin (NIM)
kpis.formula: ((Interest Income - Interest Expense) / Average Earning Assets) * 100

kpis.name: Cost of Funds
kpis.formula: Total Interest Expense / Average Interest-Bearing Liabilities

kpis.name: Loan-to-Deposit Ratio
kpis.formula: (Total Loans / Total Deposits) * 100

kpis.name: Mortgage Cost-to-Close
kpis.formula: Total Origination Cost / Closed Loan Volume (count)

kpis.name: Mortgage Cycle Time (Days to Close)
kpis.formula: Average Days from Application to Funding

kpis.name: Mortgage Pull-Through Rate
kpis.formula: (Funded Loans / Approved Applications) * 100

kpis.name: AML Alert False Positive Rate
kpis.formula: (False Positive Alerts / Total Alerts Generated) * 100

kpis.name: SAR Filing Timeliness
kpis.formula: (SARs Filed Within 30 Days / Total SARs Required) * 100

kpis.name: Cost Per AML Alert
kpis.formula: Total AML Operations Cost / Total Alerts Reviewed

kpis.name: Claims Leakage Ratio
kpis.formula: ((Actual Claim Payout - Target Claim Payout) / Target Claim Payout) * 100

kpis.name: Loss Adjustment Expense (LAE) Ratio
kpis.formula: (Loss Adjustment Expenses / Net Earned Premium) * 100

kpis.name: Claims Cycle Time
kpis.formula: Average Days from FNOL to Payment

kpis.name: Combined Ratio
kpis.formula: Loss Ratio + Expense Ratio (or (Incurred Losses + LAE + UW Expenses) / Net Earned Premium) * 100

kpis.name: Expense Ratio
kpis.formula: (Underwriting Expenses / Net Written Premium) * 100

kpis.name: Catastrophe Load
kpis.formula: (Cat-Related Incurred Losses / Net Earned Premium) * 100

kpis.name: Effective Fee Rate on AUM
kpis.formula: (Total Revenue / Average AUM) * 100

kpis.name: Net Flow Rate (AUM)
kpis.formula: ((Inflows - Outflows) / Beginning AUM) * 100

kpis.name: Revenue Per Advisor
kpis.formula: Total Advisory Revenue / Number of Advisors

kpis.name: Settlement Fail Rate
kpis.formula: (Failed Trades / Total Trades) * 100

kpis.name: Trade Matching Automation Rate
kpis.formula: (Auto-Matched Trades / Total Trades) * 100

kpis.name: Cost Per Trade Settled
kpis.formula: Total Settlement Operations Cost / Trades Settled

kpis.name: Mean Time to Identify (MTTI)
kpis.formula: Average Hours from Breach Occurrence to Detection

kpis.name: Mean Time to Contain (MTTC)
kpis.formula: Average Hours from Detection to Containment

kpis.name: Cybersecurity Investment Ratio
kpis.formula: (Cybersecurity Spend / Total IT Spend) * 100

kpis.name: Regulatory Filing Timeliness Rate
kpis.formula: (Filings Submitted On-Time / Total Required Filings) * 100

kpis.name: Data Quality Exception Rate
kpis.formula: (Fields with Quality Exceptions / Total Fields Submitted) * 100

kpis.name: Cost Per Regulatory Filing
kpis.formula: Total Regulatory Reporting Cost / Number of Distinct Filings

kpis.name: Payment Fraud Loss Rate
kpis.formula: (Fraud Losses / Total Payment Volume) * 100

kpis.name: Fraud Detection Rate
kpis.formula: (Detected Fraud Attempts / Total Fraud Attempts) * 100

kpis.name: Customer Reimbursement Rate (APP Scams)
kpis.formula: (Reimbursed APP Fraud Losses / Total APP Fraud Losses) * 100

kpis.name: SMB Loan Time-to-Decision
kpis.formula: Average Days from Application to Credit Decision

kpis.name: SMB Loan Application Abandonment Rate
kpis.formula: (Abandoned Applications / Total Applications Started) * 100

kpis.name: SMB Loan Approval Rate
kpis.formula: (Approved Applications / Total Applications Submitted) * 100

kpis.name: Non-Performing Loan (NPL) Ratio
kpis.formula: (Non-Performing Loans / Total Gross Loans) * 100

kpis.name: Provision for Credit Losses (PCL) Ratio
kpis.formula: (Provision for Credit Losses / Average Net Loans) * 100

kpis.name: Watch List Loan Ratio
kpis.formula: (Watch List Loans / Total Gross Loans) * 100

kpis.name: AUM Per Advisor
kpis.formula: Total AUM / Number of Financial Advisors

kpis.name: Advisor Productivity Growth
kpis.formula: ((Current Period Revenue Per Advisor - Prior Period Revenue Per Advisor) / Prior Period Revenue Per Advisor) * 100

kpis.name: Client-to-Advisor Ratio
kpis.formula: Total Number of Clients / Number of Advisors

kpis.name: BNPL Net Loss Rate
kpis.formula: (Charge-offs - Recoveries) / Average Receivables * 100

kpis.name: BNPL Repeat Usage Rate
kpis.formula: (Customers with >1 Transaction / Total Active Customers) * 100

kpis.name: BNPL Collection Efficiency
kpis.formula: (Collections Made / Collections Expected) * 100

kpis.name: Critical Vendor Concentration
kpis.formula: (Spend with Top 3 Vendors / Total Third-Party Spend) * 100

kpis.name: Vendor Risk Assessment Coverage
kpis.formula: (Vendors Assessed / Total Active Vendors) * 100

kpis.name: Third-Party Incident Response Time
kpis.formula: Average Hours from Vendor Incident Notification to Containment

kpis.name: Core Deposit Growth Rate
kpis.formula: ((Current Core Deposits - Prior Core Deposits) / Prior Core Deposits) * 100

kpis.name: Customer Churn Rate
kpis.formula: (Customers Lost / Average Customers) * 100

kpis.name: Deposit Beta (Customer Retention Sensitivity)
kpis.formula: (Rate-Sensitive Deposit Outflows / Total Rate-Sensitive Deposits) * 100

kpis.name: Model Validation Cycle Time
kpis.formula: Average Days from Model Submission to Validation Sign-off

kpis.name: Model Risk Findings Open Rate
kpis.formula: (Open MRM Findings / Total Findings Raised) * 100

kpis.name: AI/ML Model Governance Coverage
kpis.formula: (GoverneD AI Models / Total AI Models in Production) * 100

kpis.name: Cross-Border Payment Settlement Time
kpis.formula: Average Hours from Initiation to Beneficiary Credit

kpis.name: FX Spread Revenue
kpis.formula: (FX Transaction Revenue / FX Volume) * 10000

kpis.name: Cross-Border Payment Inquiry Rate
kpis.formula: (Payment Inquiries / Total Cross-Border Payments) * 100

kpis.name: Digital Distribution Share
kpis.formula: (Digital-Originated Premium / Total Gross Written Premium) * 100

kpis.name: Quote-to-Bind Cycle Time
kpis.formula: Average Hours from Quote to Policy Binding

kpis.name: Agent Commission Ratio
kpis.formula: (Total Commissions Paid / Net Earned Premium) * 100

kpis.name: Data Quality Score
kpis.formula: (Compliant Data Fields / Total Data Fields) * 100

kpis.name: Data Lineage Coverage
kpis.formula: (Data Elements with Documented Lineage / Total Data Elements) * 100

kpis.name: AI Model Data Provenance Rate
kpis.formula: (AI Models Using Certified Data / Total AI Models) * 100

kpis.name: Actuarial Close Cycle Time
kpis.formula: Business Days from Month-End to Final Actuarial Report

kpis.name: IFRS 17 Reporting Accuracy
kpis.formula: (Correct IFRS 17 Entries / Total IFRS 17 Entries) * 100

kpis.name: Actuarial System Modernization Index
kpis.formula: (Modernized Processes / Total Actuarial Processes) * 100

kpis.name: Reconciliation Break Age
kpis.formula: Average Days Outstanding for Unreconciled Breaks

kpis.name: Fund Administration Cost Ratio
kpis.formula: (Fund Admin Costs / AUM) * 10000

kpis.name: Investor Reporting Cycle Time
kpis.formula: Days from Month-End to Investor Statement Delivery

kpis.name: Digital Asset Custody Compliance Score
kpis.formula: (Compliant Custody Processes / Total Custody Processes) * 100

kpis.name: Stablecoin Reserve Coverage Ratio
kpis.formula: (Reserves Held / Stablecoins Issued) * 100

kpis.name: Regulatory License Coverage (Digital Assets)
kpis.formula: (Jurisdictions with Required Licenses / Total Operating Jurisdictions) * 100

valueDrivers.signalPattern: Legacy core system age >15 years + maintenance ratio >40% of IT budget
Run-the-bank IT spend >65% of total IT budget
Product launch cycle >9 months for new deposit/loan products
CAC >$250 per retail customer with digital conversion <2%
CLV/CAC ratio <3x with churn >15% annually
NIM declining >15bps YoY with deposit beta >70%
Cost of funds rising faster than loan yield with LDR >95%
Cost-to-close >$8,000 with cycle time >45 days
Pull-through rate <65% with rework rate >12%
AML false positive rate >90% with alert backlog >30 days
SAR filing timeliness <90% with regulatory MRAs on BSA/AML
Claims leakage >8% with LAE ratio >12%
Claims cycle time >15 days for auto with subrogation <40%
Combined ratio >100 with expense ratio >30
Cat load >10 points with retention declining on renewals
Effective fee rate <35bps with 3 consecutive quarters of net outflows
ETF AUM growing >20% while mutual fund AUM declining
Settlement fail rate >5% with CSDR penalties >$10M
Manual matching >20% with operations headcount growing faster than volume
Cyber incidents >1 per year with MTTC >30 days
Cyber insurance premiums >$5M with unplanned security spend >15% of IT budget
>2 restatements in 12 months with filings within 48h of deadline
Regulatory reporting headcount >150 FTEs with manual ETL processes
Fraud losses >0.30% of payment volume with APP scam reimbursement >50%
Fraud detection latency >500ms on real-time rails
SMB time-to-decision >10 days with abandonment >60%
SMB approval rate <30% with cost-to-decide >$2,000
NPL ratio >1.5% with watch list loans growing >20%
CECL provision >2% of loans with earnings volatility from model sensitivity
Average advisor age >55 with AUM per advisor <$75M
Client-to-advisor ratio >150 with revenue per advisor declining
BNPL net loss rate >8% with repeat usage >70%
Collection costs >15% of recoveries with funding spread widening
>50% of critical functions outsourced with vendor incidents >2/year
Due diligence cycle >90 days with no real-time vendor monitoring
Deposit beta >60% with core deposit outflows >5% quarterly
CD renewal rate <50% with customer churn >15% annually
Model inventory >500 with validation backlog >6 months
AI models in production without validation with governance coverage <70%
Cross-border settlement >2 days with FX spread >100bps for SMB
Payment inquiry volume >20% of ops with SWIFT gpi adoption <80%
New business from agents <40% with digital direct growing >25%
Quote bind issue cycle >48 hours with agent commission >15%
>30% data fields with unknown ownership and quality score <70%
AI models using ungoverned data with data remediation >$10M annually
Close cycle >15 business days with actuarial system age >15 years
IFRS 17 implementation over budget with data reconciliation >5 days
Operations headcount >20% of total with reconciliation breaks >3 days old
Investor reporting cycle >10 days with technology spend >20% of non-comp opex
No qualified custodian status with reserve attestation gaps
State MSB license fragmentation with SEC Wells notice received

formulas.name: Fraud Loss Reduction Value
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH when historical loss data >24 months; MEDIUM when using industry benchmarks; LOW for greenfield digital asset platforms

formulas.name: Mortgage Cost-to-Close Reduction
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH when LOS data available; MEDIUM when using industry average cost; LOW for non-delegated correspondent channels

formulas.name: NIM Expansion from Deposit Beta Improvement
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with ALCO model access; MEDIUM with peer benchmark; LOW in extreme rate volatility
formulas.exampleCalculation: (0.55 - 0.75) * -0.02 * $40B * 0.90 = $144M annual pre-tax improvement (beta reduction from 75% to 55%)

formulas.name: AML False Positive Reduction Savings
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with 12+ months alert history; MEDIUM with vendor benchmark; LOW for new market entrants

formulas.name: Claims Leakage Reduction Value
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with claims audit data; MEDIUM using industry leakage benchmarks; LOW for specialty lines with limited data

formulas.name: Combined Ratio Improvement from Underwriting Automation
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with line-level data; MEDIUM using segment averages; LOW for catastrophe-exposed lines

formulas.name: AUM Growth from Advisor Productivity
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with platform usage data; MEDIUM with industry productivity curves; LOW during market downturns

formulas.name: Settlement Fail Penalty Avoidance
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with DTCC fail data; MEDIUM with CSDR estimate; LOW for non-CSDR jurisdictions

formulas.name: Cyber Breach Cost Avoidance
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with incident history; MEDIUM using IBM breach cost benchmarks; LOW for zero-incident firms

formulas.name: Regulatory Reporting Automation Savings
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with current state process mapping; MEDIUM using benchmark FTE ratios; LOW for newly regulated entities

formulas.name: Customer Churn Reduction Value
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with cohort retention data; MEDIUM using industry churn benchmarks; LOW for new product lines

formulas.name: SMB Lending Revenue Uplift from Speed
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with pipeline data; MEDIUM using SBA benchmark; LOW for new geographic markets

formulas.name: Credit Risk Provisioning Optimization
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with CECL model access; MEDIUM with peer provisioning comparison; LOW during economic inflection points

formulas.name: Core Deposit Growth Value
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with deposit repricing model; MEDIUM with peer comparison; LOW in rate-cutting cycles

formulas.name: Model Risk Management Efficiency
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with MRM function data; MEDIUM with benchmark; LOW for firms with <50 models

formulas.name: Cross-Border Payment Revenue Uplift
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with treasury client P&L; MEDIUM using industry FX benchmark; LOW for new corridor launches

formulas.name: Insurance Distribution Cost Reduction
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with channel economics; MEDIUM using broker benchmark; LOW for regulated lines

formulas.name: Data Governance Cost Avoidance
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with data quality audit; MEDIUM using benchmark; LOW for firms with <5% data spend

formulas.name: Actuarial Transformation ROI
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with close process costed; MEDIUM using industry benchmark; LOW for mutual insurers

formulas.name: Hedge Fund Operational Alpha
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with ops data; MEDIUM using administrator benchmark; LOW for sub-$500M funds

formulas.name: Third-Party Risk Cost Avoidance
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with incident history; MEDIUM using industry TPRM benchmark; LOW for firms with <20 critical vendors

formulas.name: Payment Fraud APP Scam Reduction
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with APP loss data; MEDIUM using UK PSR benchmark; LOW for markets without APP reimbursement rules

formulas.name: BNPL Portfolio Profitability Improvement
formulas.confidenceRules: MEDIUM with vintage curves; LOW in high-growth phase; HIGH with 24+ month vintage data

formulas.name: CET1 Capital Efficiency Improvement
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with RWA model access; MEDIUM using standardized approach; LOW for advanced IRB transitions

formulas.name: Wealth Management Platform Efficiency
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with time-motion study; MEDIUM using benchmark advisor productivity; LOW for hybrid advisor models

benchmarks.name: Cybersecurity Investment Ratio (Financial Services)
benchmarks.source: IBM Cost of Data Breach Report 2024

benchmarks.name: Average Cost of Data Breach (Financial Services)
benchmarks.source: IBM Cost of Data Breach Report 2024

benchmarks.name: Mean Time to Identify Breach (Financial Services)
benchmarks.source: IBM Cost of Data Breach Report 2024

benchmarks.name: BNPL Net Loss Rate (Industry)
benchmarks.source: CB Insights State of Fintech Q2 2024

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Technology and communication expenses growing >10% YoY while digital revenue <20% of total
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: IT investment is not translating to digital revenue growth, suggesting legacy maintenance burden or failed transformation

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Core banking platform contract renewal >$100M annually with >15 year system age
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: High vendor lock-in with legacy architecture; modernization likely constrained by contractual and technical debt

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Deposit beta increased >20bps in single quarter with CD promotional rates rising
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Rate-sensitive depositors migrating to higher-yield alternatives; funding cost pressure intensifying

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Mortgage servicing rights sold or subserviced >30% of portfolio in 12 months
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Origination economics unfavorable or capacity constraints forcing strategic retreat from direct servicing

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Regulatory consent order or MRA within 24 months mentioning transaction monitoring or SAR timeliness
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: AML program deficiencies confirmed by regulator; elevated risk of penalties and mandated remediation spend

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Litigation reserves growing >25% YoY with claims severity exceeding prior year by >10%
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Claims management process gaps leading to adverse development; potential leakage and reserve adequacy concerns

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Combined ratio >100 for 2+ consecutive quarters with expense ratio >30
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Underwriting profitability eroding; management likely considering rate increases, expense reductions, or reinsurance restructuring

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: 3+ consecutive quarters of net outflows with effective fee rate declining >5bps annually
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Product-market fit erosion; active management value proposition under pressure from passive alternatives

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Settlement fail rate increased >3x baseline in 90 days following T+1 transition
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Operational infrastructure not ready for accelerated settlement; penalty and reputational risk elevated

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: SEC Form 8-K filed for cybersecurity incident with materiality determination
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Material cyber incident confirmed; remediation costs, regulatory scrutiny, and litigation exposure likely

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: 8-K filed for financial restatement or material weakness in ICFR (internal controls)
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Control environment failure; SOX compliance gap with potential SEC enforcement and auditor rotation risk

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: CFPB complaints increasing >50% YoY with fraud/scam category leading
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Customer harm from fraud processes; regulatory investigation likely; reputational and remediation cost risk

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: SBA 7(a) originations declining >15% YoY while fintech SMB lending growing >30%
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Competitive displacement in core SMB segment; digital speed and experience gaps driving market share loss

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: CRE loans >300% of risk-based capital with classified CRE >5% of portfolio
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Concentration risk at regulatory threshold; potential provisioning acceleration and capital constraint

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Advisory headcount declining >5% annually with average advisor age >55
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Succession crisis in wealth management; AUM attrition risk as advisors retire without adequate replacement pipeline

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: CFPB or state AG inquiry into BNPL products with Wells notice or civil investigative demand
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Product structure under regulatory scrutiny; potential forced underwriting changes, fee restrictions, or consent decree

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Single vendor outage affecting >20% of customer-facing services for >4 hours
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Concentration risk materialized; business continuity and TPRM program gaps exposed

signalRules.signalName: Deposit Outflow to Money Market Funds
signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Core deposit outflows >5% quarterly with brokered deposits increasing >20%
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Flight to higher-yield alternatives; liquidity risk elevating; funding mix deteriorating

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Public statements about AI/ML deployment with no mention of model risk governance in 10-K risk factors
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: AI adoption outpacing governance; potential SR 11-7 gaps and regulatory criticism risk

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Cross-border payment revenue declining >10% with correspondent banking relationships reduced >15%
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: De-risking trend or competitive displacement; friction in correspondent network reducing client value proposition

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Insurance carrier reducing agent commissions >10% or shifting to digital-only products
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Distribution strategy pivot; potential channel conflict and agent attrition risk

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Internal audit or regulator identifying BCBS 239 gaps in data aggregation or reporting
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Data governance foundation inadequate for risk reporting; potential regulatory penalty and AI deployment constraint

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: IFRS 17 implementation pushed >6 months with disclosed budget overrun >30%
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Actuarial and finance transformation complexity underestimated; potential reporting deadline risk and cost escalation

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Redemption notices >15% of AUM in single quarter with lock-up expirations approaching
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Investor confidence erosion; potential liquidity stress and operational scaling challenges

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Change in reserve attestation auditor with qualified opinion or delayed attestation
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Reserve transparency concerns; potential regulatory action and counterparty confidence erosion

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Compliance-related job postings increased >40% in 90 days with senior roles predominating
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Regulatory pressure or internal control failure driving hiring; likely pain point in AML, fraud, or regulatory reporting

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Management mentions 'legacy system' or 'technical debt' with negative sentiment score on earnings call
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Leadership acknowledging infrastructure constraint; potential budget reallocation or RFP activity incoming

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Public cloud migration announcement with >3 year timeline and disclosed budget >$100M
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Major infrastructure transformation in progress; integration, data governance, and operational risk during transition

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Moody's or S&P placing issuer on negative watch with specific mention of asset quality or capital
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Fundamental credit deterioration; potential funding cost increase, collateral calls, and counterparty relationship stress

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: CRO, CCO, or GC departure within 12 months of regulatory action or MRA
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Control environment leadership instability; potential cultural issue or board dissatisfaction with risk management

evidenceSources.name: SEC 10-K / 10-Q Filings
evidenceSources.description: Annual and quarterly reports containing financial performance, risk factors, IT spend, litigation, and material weakness disclosures.

evidenceSources.name: FFIEC Call Reports (UBPR)
evidenceSources.description: Uniform Bank Performance Report with granular balance sheet, income statement, and performance ratios for US banks.

evidenceSources.name: NAIC Annual Statement Database
evidenceSources.description: Statutory financial filings for US insurers including loss triangles, premium, and reserve data.
evidenceSources.accessMethod: NAIC SBS Database / S&P Global Market Intelligence

evidenceSources.name: Earnings Call Transcripts
evidenceSources.description: Quarterly management commentary revealing strategic priorities, pain acknowledgment, and guidance changes.

evidenceSources.name: Job Posting Analytics
evidenceSources.description: Hiring velocity, role types, and skill requirements indicating technology and capability gaps.
evidenceSources.accessMethod: LinkedIn, Indeed, Burning Glass, Revelio Labs

evidenceSources.name: Regulatory Enforcement Database
evidenceSources.description: Consent orders, civil money penalties, MRAs, and examination findings from prudential regulators.
evidenceSources.accessMethod: OCC, FDIC, Federal Reserve, CFPB, FINRA, state DOI websites

evidenceSources.name: Technology Vendor Contract Databases
evidenceSources.description: Public procurement records and contract awards revealing system age, vendor concentration, and renewal timing.

evidenceSources.name: Industry Benchmark Surveys
evidenceSources.description: Anonymized peer comparison data from consulting and industry associations.
evidenceSources.accessMethod: McKinsey, Deloitte, Accenture, PwC, EY, Celent, Aite-Novarica

evidenceSources.name: Patent and Technology Disclosure Filings
evidenceSources.description: Patent applications and technology partnership announcements revealing innovation investment areas.
evidenceSources.accessMethod: USPTO, company press releases, conference presentations

evidenceSources.name: Fund Administrator and Custody Reports
evidenceSources.description: Form ADV, PF filings, and custody statements revealing AUM, fee rates, and operational structure.
evidenceSources.accessMethod: SEC IAPD, Form PF, administrator public disclosures

evidenceSources.name: Cybersecurity Incident Databases
evidenceSources.description: Data breach notifications, SEC 8-K cyber disclosures, and FS-ISAC intelligence.
evidenceSources.accessMethod: SEC EDGAR, state AG breach notices, FS-ISAC

evidenceSources.name: Social and Executive Sentiment Analysis
evidenceSources.description: Glassdoor ratings, LinkedIn activity, and executive statements indicating cultural and strategic signals.

buyingTriggers.name: Regulatory Consent Order Issuance
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Formal regulatory consent order or cease-and-desist order issued with technology remediation requirements
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Accelerated RFP with compliance-driven vendor selection; budget typically pre-approved for remediation

buyingTriggers.name: Core Banking Contract Expiration
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Core banking platform contract entering final 18-24 months of term
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Multi-year evaluation cycle; competitive RFP likely; migration cost and risk are key criteria

buyingTriggers.name: New Product Launch Window
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Strategic plan committing to new digital product or market entry within 12 months
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Speed-to-market prioritized; vendor must demonstrate referenceable deployment timeline

buyingTriggers.name: CEO/CIO Change
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: New executive appointed with public mandate for digital transformation or cost reduction
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: New executive often brings preferred vendor relationships; early engagement critical

buyingTriggers.name: Post-Merger Integration
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Acquisition closure requiring platform consolidation and cost synergy delivery
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Synergy-driven procurement; vendor must demonstrate integration expertise and speed

buyingTriggers.name: Cybersecurity Material Incident
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Material breach or ransomware attack requiring immediate security investment
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Emergency budget allocation; CISO/CIO direct authority; speed and reference critical

buyingTriggers.name: NIM Compression Crisis
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: NIM decline >25bps in single quarter triggering ALCO action and expense review
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Cost reduction focus; ROI must show near-term P&L impact; vendor financing may help

buyingTriggers.name: AUM Net Outflow Crisis
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: 3+ consecutive quarters of net outflows with fee rate compression
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Revenue protection focus; vendor must show distribution or performance improvement path

buyingTriggers.name: IFRS 17 / LDTI Deadline
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Accounting standard effective date approaching with implementation gaps remaining
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Compliance-driven; vendor must demonstrate successful implementation references

buyingTriggers.name: T+1 Settlement Transition
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Industry-mandated settlement cycle compression requiring operational retooling
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Industry coordination required; vendor must be DTCC/NSCC certified or compatible

buyingTriggers.name: AML Program Independent Review
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Regulatory requirement for independent AML program review or look-back
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Vendor must have look-back experience; fixed-fee or outcome-based pricing preferred

buyingTriggers.name: Customer Churn Spike
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Customer attrition >2x baseline with NPS decline >10 points
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Experience and retention focus; proof points from similar turnarounds valued

buyingTriggers.name: Fintech Competitive Entry
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: New fintech entrant capturing >5% market share in core product line within 12 months
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Speed-to-market critical; incumbent vendor must match fintech capability claims

buyingTriggers.name: Model Risk Management Examination Findings
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Regulatory examination with MRM deficiencies requiring remediation
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Vendor must demonstrate SR 11-7 / OCC model risk expertise; regulatory references required

buyingTriggers.name: Capital Efficiency Pressure (CET1)
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: CET1 ratio approaching buffer minimum or RWA inflation >15%
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: CFO-driven; must demonstrate capital relief or RWA optimization; risk quantification essential

buyingTriggers.name: Data Center / Cloud Migration Mandate
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Board or CEO mandate to exit owned data centers within 24-36 months
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Cloud-native or hybrid cloud capability required; security and compliance certification critical

buyingTriggers.name: Wealth Advisor Succession Event
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Senior advisor retirement wave (>10% of AUM-linked advisors departing within 24 months)
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Advisory platform and digital client engagement focus; advisor recruitment tool integration valued

buyingTriggers.name: Third-Party Vendor Incident
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Critical vendor outage or breach affecting >10% of revenue or operations
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: TPRM platform urgency; real-time monitoring and resilience requirements; board-level visibility

buyingTriggers.name: BNPL Regulatory Rule Finalization
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: CFPB or state finalizes BNPL regulation requiring underwriting or disclosure changes
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Compliance deadline driven; vendor must demonstrate rapid implementation track record

buyingTriggers.name: Digital Asset Custody License Requirement
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: OCC, SEC, or state requires qualified custodian status for digital asset products
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Regulatory certification mandatory; vendor must have approved custody infrastructure

buyingTriggers.name: Claims Severity Inflation Event
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Catastrophe or social inflation driving claims severity >20% above prior year
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Claims analytics and fraud detection urgency; vendor must demonstrate catastrophe response capability

buyingTriggers.name: SOX Material Weakness Disclosure
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: 10-K discloses material weakness in ICFR requiring remediation
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Audit committee visibility; controls automation and GRC platform urgency; Big 4 involvement likely

buyingTriggers.name: Payment Rail Modernization (FedNow/RTP)
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Competitive or regulatory pressure to adopt real-time payment rails
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Real-time fraud detection and treasury integration requirements; vendor must be RTP/FedNow certified

buyingTriggers.name: Hedge Fund Redemption Gate Activation
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Fund activates redemption gate or extends notice period due to liquidity stress
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Investor reporting and transparency urgency; administrator change possible; operational audit likely

buyingTriggers.name: Cross-Border De-Risking Event
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Correspondent banking relationship terminated by partner bank or regulator
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Alternative payment infrastructure urgency; compliance and sanctions screening critical; SWIFT gpi or blockchain solutions considered

personas.name: Chief Financial Officer (CFO)
dislikedClaims: Vague efficiency promises without dollar amounts
Vendor case studies from unrelated industries

personas.name: Chief Risk Officer (CRO)
pressures: Model risk and AI governance gaps
trustedEvidence: Academic and industry research on risk models
dislikedClaims: AI replacing human judgment without governance
Vendors without financial services domain expertise

personas.name: Chief Compliance Officer (CCO)
goals: Reduce compliance cost per dollar of revenue

personas.name: Chief Operating Officer (COO)
dislikedClaims: Solutions requiring process redesign without change management

personas.name: Chief Information Security Officer (CISO)
pressures: Ransomware and supply chain threat escalation
trustedEvidence: IBM Cost of Data Breach data
dislikedClaims: Vendors without financial services security references

personas.name: Chief Marketing Officer (CMO) / Chief Digital Officer
dislikedClaims: Generic B2C case studies applied to financial services

personas.name: Head of Digital / Chief Digital Officer
Chief Claims Officer
dislikedClaims: Fraud detection without false positive control
Generic claims solutions without line expertise

personas.name: Chief Underwriting Officer
dislikedClaims: Rate adequacy without loss cost analysis
AI pricing without regulatory filing capability

personas.name: Chief Data Officer (CDO)
dislikedClaims: Generic data quality without financial services context

personas.name: Chief Actuary
dislikedClaims: Black box models without actuarial override

personas.name: General Counsel / Chief Legal Officer
goals: Advise on M&A and strategic transactions

discoveryQuestions.question: What percentage of your IT budget is currently consumed by run-the-bank versus change-the-bank activities, and how has that shifted over the past three years?
discoveryQuestions.expectedInsight: Quantifies legacy maintenance burden and transformation capacity constraint

discoveryQuestions.question: How many months does it typically take to launch a new retail deposit or lending product from concept to market, and where do the bottlenecks occur?
discoveryQuestions.expectedInsight: Reveals core system agility constraints and product innovation friction

discoveryQuestions.question: What is your current all-in cost to acquire a new retail customer, and how has that trended as digital competitors have scaled?
discoveryQuestions.expectedInsight: Quantifies CAC escalation and digital channel efficiency gaps

discoveryQuestions.question: Walk me through your current mortgage origination process from application to funding. What is your cost-to-close and average cycle time, and how do you compare to the top quartile?
discoveryQuestions.expectedInsight: Exposes LOS inefficiency, manual process bloat, and competitive positioning

discoveryQuestions.question: What is your current AML alert false positive rate, and how many investigator FTEs are required to clear your monthly alert volume?
discoveryQuestions.expectedInsight: Quantifies AML program inefficiency and human capital constraint

discoveryQuestions.question: In your claims organization, what percentage of incurred losses would you attribute to leakage, and what is your average loss adjustment expense ratio by line?
discoveryQuestions.expectedInsight: Reveals claims process quality and cost containment opportunity

discoveryQuestions.question: What is your current combined ratio by major line of business, and what are the top three drivers preventing you from reaching your target?
discoveryQuestions.expectedInsight: Identifies pricing, expense, or catastrophe pressure points

discoveryQuestions.question: How has your effective fee rate on AUM trended over the past three years, and what proportion of your net flows are going to passive versus active strategies?
discoveryQuestions.expectedInsight: Quantifies fee compression and product mix shift dynamics

discoveryQuestions.question: What is your current settlement fail rate, and how much are you incurring in CSDR penalties or manual reconciliation costs annually?
discoveryQuestions.expectedInsight: Exposes post-T+1 operational readiness and cost leakage

discoveryQuestions.question: When was your last material cybersecurity incident, and what was your mean time to identify and contain? How does that compare to industry benchmarks?
discoveryQuestions.expectedInsight: Reveals security posture maturity and incident response capability

discoveryQuestions.question: How many regulatory restatements or material weaknesses have you experienced in the past 24 months, and what is the fully-loaded cost per regulatory filing?
discoveryQuestions.expectedInsight: Quantifies control environment weakness and reporting cost burden

discoveryQuestions.question: What is your current fraud loss rate as a percentage of payment volume, and how much are you reimbursing customers for APP scams?
discoveryQuestions.expectedInsight: Exposes real-time payment fraud exposure and customer reimbursement cost

discoveryQuestions.question: For your small business lending segment, what is your average time-to-decision, application abandonment rate, and cost-to-decide per loan?
discoveryQuestions.expectedInsight: Reveals competitive speed gaps and unit economics in SMB lending

discoveryQuestions.question: What is your current NPL ratio, and how concentrated is your portfolio in commercial real estate? What is your watch list loan trend?
discoveryQuestions.expectedInsight: Quantifies credit quality deterioration and concentration risk

discoveryQuestions.question: What is the average age of your advisor force, and what is your current AUM per advisor? How many advisors are expected to retire in the next five years?
discoveryQuestions.expectedInsight: Exposes succession risk and productivity improvement opportunity

discoveryQuestions.question: How many models are currently in your production inventory, and what is your average validation cycle time? Are any AI/ML models operating without validation?
discoveryQuestions.expectedInsight: Reveals model risk governance maturity and AI deployment gaps

discoveryQuestions.question: What percentage of your critical business functions are outsourced, and how many vendor incidents have you experienced in the past 12 months?
discoveryQuestions.expectedInsight: Quantifies third-party concentration and operational resilience gaps

discoveryQuestions.question: What is your current core deposit growth rate versus your cost of funds trend? How much deposit outflow have you experienced to higher-yield alternatives?
discoveryQuestions.expectedInsight: Reveals funding stability and NIM pressure dynamics

discoveryQuestions.question: What is your current data quality score, and what percentage of your data elements have documented ownership and lineage?
discoveryQuestions.expectedInsight: Exposes data governance foundation gaps and AI readiness

discoveryQuestions.question: How many business days does your actuarial close cycle require, and what is your current IFRS 17 implementation status?
discoveryQuestions.expectedInsight: Reveals actuarial transformation urgency and accounting standard readiness

objections.objectionText: We already have a vendor/partner for this capability.
objections.reframeApproach: Acknowledge incumbent, then focus on incremental capability gaps or total cost of ownership comparison. Propose pilot on non-overlapping use case.

objections.objectionText: Our budget is frozen for this fiscal year.
objections.reframeApproach: Quantify cost of inaction in same-fiscal-year terms. Explore OPEX-to-CAPEX conversion, vendor financing, or cost-neutral models funded by savings.

objections.objectionText: We need to finish our current transformation before evaluating new vendors.
objections.reframeApproach: Position as accelerator or de-risker for current program, not replacement. Show integration with current roadmap and how it reduces project risk.

objections.objectionText: Our regulators would never approve this approach.
objections.reframeApproach: Cite regulator-blessed precedents or supervisory guidance that supports the approach. Offer regulatory engagement support and documented compliance mapping.

objections.objectionText: We have an internal team building this.
objections.reframeApproach: Compare time-to-value and total cost of ownership. Highlight opportunity cost of internal development and reference successful build-then-buy transitions.

objections.objectionText: The business case ROI is not compelling enough.
objections.reframeApproach: Recalculate with risk-adjusted value including avoided losses, regulatory penalties, and reputational damage. Include sensitivity analysis and peer benchmarking.

objections.objectionText: We tried something similar before and it failed.
objections.reframeApproach: Acknowledge failure, diagnose root cause (typically change management, not technology), and present differentiated approach with explicit risk mitigation.

objections.objectionText: Our data is too messy/complex for this to work.
objections.reframeApproach: Position data remediation as part of solution, not prerequisite. Reference data quality improvement as explicit deliverable with governance framework.

objections.objectionText: We need to see reference customers in our exact segment and size.
objections.reframeApproach: Provide closest references with segment adjacency rationale. Offer site visit or direct reference call. If early market, offer risk-sharing pricing.

objections.objectionText: The integration with our legacy systems would be too complex.
objections.reframeApproach: Present integration accelerator or middleware approach. Offer proof-of-concept on most complex integration point with dedicated integration team.

technologySystems.name: Core Banking Platform
technologySystems.description: Primary ledger and transaction processing system for deposits, loans, and payments.

technologySystems.name: Loan Origination System (LOS)
technologySystems.description: End-to-end loan application, underwriting, and closing workflow platform.

technologySystems.name: Anti-Money Laundering (AML) / Transaction Monitoring
technologySystems.description: Suspicious activity detection, alert generation, and case management for BSA/AML compliance.

technologySystems.name: Customer Relationship Management (CRM)
technologySystems.description: Client data management, sales pipeline, and service interaction tracking.

technologySystems.name: Wealth Management Platform
technologySystems.description: Advisor workstation, portfolio management, rebalancing, and client reporting.

technologySystems.name: Policy Administration System (PAS)
technologySystems.description: Policy issuance, billing, endorsements, and servicing for insurance carriers.

technologySystems.name: Claims Management System
technologySystems.description: First notice of loss, claim workflow, reserve management, and payment processing.

technologySystems.name: Trading and Order Management System (OMS/EMS)
technologySystems.description: Order routing, execution, position management, and pre/post-trade compliance.

technologySystems.name: Portfolio Accounting / Investment Book of Record (IBOR)
technologySystems.description: Investment positions, transactions, corporate actions, and NAV calculation.

technologySystems.name: Payment Hub / Real-Time Payments
technologySystems.description: Payment routing, orchestration, and connectivity to RTP, FedNow, SWIFT, and card networks.

technologySystems.name: Treasury Management System (TMS)
technologySystems.description: Cash positioning, liquidity forecasting, payments, and bank connectivity.

technologySystems.name: General Ledger / ERP
technologySystems.description: Financial accounting, consolidation, and management reporting system.

technologySystems.name: Regulatory Reporting Platform
technologySystems.description: Data aggregation, validation, and submission for regulatory filings.

technologySystems.name: Data Warehouse / Lakehouse
technologySystems.description: Enterprise data consolidation, analytics, and AI/ML enablement platform.

technologySystems.name: GRC / Audit Management Platform
technologySystems.description: Governance, risk, and controls management with audit workflow and issue tracking.

technologySystems.name: Cybersecurity / SIEM
technologySystems.description: Security information and event management with threat detection and incident response.

technologySystems.name: Fraud Detection Platform
technologySystems.description: Real-time and batch fraud detection across channels with adaptive machine learning.

technologySystems.name: Digital Banking / Mobile Platform
technologySystems.description: Customer-facing mobile and web banking with account opening, payments, and servicing.

technologySystems.name: Enterprise Risk Management (ERM)
technologySystems.description: Aggregated risk identification, assessment, and monitoring across credit, market, and operational risk.

technologySystems.name: Actuarial Modeling Platform
technologySystems.description: Reserve valuation, pricing models, and financial projection for insurance liabilities.

regulatoryFactors.name: Basel III Endgame / Basel IV
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Internationally active banks; US implementation via Federal Reserve/OCC/FDIC
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Capital constraint, business restriction, enforcement action, rating downgrade

regulatoryFactors.name: Dodd-Frank Act Stress Tests (DFAST) / CCAR
regulatoryFactors.regulation: Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Capital plan rejection, growth restriction, M&A moratorium, enforcement

regulatoryFactors.name: Sarbanes-Oxley (SOX) 302/404
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Publicly traded companies in financial services
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: SEC enforcement, restatement, delisting risk, officer certification penalties up to $5M and 20 years imprisonment

regulatoryFactors.name: Bank Secrecy Act / Anti-Money Laundering (BSA/AML)
regulatoryFactors.regulation: 31 USC Chapter 53; 31 CFR Chapter X; FinCEN regulations
regulatoryFactors.applicability: All financial institutions and money services businesses
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Civil money penalties up to 2x transaction value; criminal penalties; consent orders; charter revocation risk

regulatoryFactors.name: Fair Lending / Equal Credit Opportunity (ECOA)
regulatoryFactors.regulation: Equal Credit Opportunity Act; Fair Housing Act
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: DOJ enforcement; CFPB consent orders; fair lending settlements ($10M+); reputation damage

regulatoryFactors.name: Consumer Financial Protection (UDAAP)
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: CFPB enforcement actions; restitution; civil penalties up to $1M per day for knowing violations

regulatoryFactors.name: GDPR / Data Protection
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Financial services firms with EU data subjects
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Fines up to 4% of global annual revenue or EUR 20M; regulatory investigation; class action exposure

regulatoryFactors.name: Gramm-Leach-Bliley (GLBA) Privacy
regulatoryFactors.regulation: GLBA Title V; FTC Safeguards Rule
regulatoryFactors.applicability: US financial institutions handling customer NPI
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: FTC enforcement; state AG actions; fines; reputational damage; customer notification costs

regulatoryFactors.name: IFRS 17 / LDTI (Insurance Accounting)
regulatoryFactors.regulation: IFRS 17 (insurance contracts); US GAAP LDTI (Long-Duration Targeted Improvements)
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Insurance entities reporting under IFRS or US GAAP
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Audit qualification; restatement; regulatory capital impact; delayed SEC filings

regulatoryFactors.name: SEC Cybersecurity Disclosure Rules (2023)
regulatoryFactors.regulation: SEC Final Rule on Cybersecurity Risk Management (2023)
regulatoryFactors.deadline: Effective September 2023; 8-K disclosure within 4 business days
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: SEC enforcement; disclosure violations; shareholder litigation; reputational damage

regulatoryFactors.name: T+1 Settlement Transition
regulatoryFactors.regulation: SEC approval; DTCC/NSCC rule changes; industry coordination
regulatoryFactors.applicability: US equity and option markets; broker-dealers; custodians
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: CSDR cash penalties; fails; operational exclusion; client dissatisfaction

regulatoryFactors.name: Real-Time Payments (FedNow / RTP)
regulatoryFactors.regulation: Federal Reserve FedNow Service; The Clearing House RTP
regulatoryFactors.applicability: US depository institutions; fintech via bank sponsors
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Competitive displacement; customer attrition; fraud exposure without real-time controls

regulatoryFactors.name: SR 11-7 / OCC Model Risk Management
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Banking organizations with material model usage
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: MRA; enforcement action; business restriction; capital impact from model deficiency

regulatoryFactors.name: MiCA (Markets in Crypto-Assets)
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Crypto-asset service providers and issuers operating in EU
regulatoryFactors.deadline: Stablecoin provisions effective June 2024; other provisions December 2024
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Fines up to EUR 10M or 2% of turnover; license revocation; market exclusion

regulatoryFactors.name: Section 1033 (Consumer Data Access)
regulatoryFactors.regulation: CFPB Rulemaking under Dodd-Frank Section 1033 (open banking)
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Banks, credit unions, fintechs providing consumer financial data
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: CFPB enforcement; compliance orders; market access restrictions

competitorFactors.name: Neobank Deposit Share Growth
competitorFactors.description: Digital-native banks (Chime, Ally, Marcus, SoFi) capturing >5% of new deposit flows with lower cost structures and superior UX.
competitorFactors.impact: Pricing pressure on deposits; CAC inflation; feature parity demands

competitorFactors.name: Fintech SMB Lending Displacement
competitorFactors.description: OnDeck, Kabbage, Fundbox, and Stripe Capital offering sub-24-hour SMB lending decisions with alternative data underwriting.
competitorFactors.impact: Traditional bank SMB share erosion; speed expectation reset; margin compression

competitorFactors.name: Passive Fund Fee Compression
competitorFactors.description: Vanguard, BlackRock iShares, and State Street driving ETF fees toward zero, pressuring active manager economics.
competitorFactors.impact: AUM outflows from active; fee rate decline; distribution restructuring

competitorFactors.name: InsurTech Digital Distribution
competitorFactors.description: Lemonade, Hippo, Root, and embedded insurance (Tesla, Amazon) bypassing traditional agent channels.
competitorFactors.impact: Agent channel obsolescence risk; direct distribution cost advantage; speed expectation reset

competitorFactors.name: Big Tech Financial Services Entry
competitorFactors.description: Apple Pay Later, Google Plex (paused), Amazon Lending, Meta payments creating ecosystem lock-in.
competitorFactors.impact: Customer relationship disintermediation; data advantage erosion; partnership or compete dilemma

competitorFactors.name: Blockchain Cross-Border Disruption
competitorFactors.description: Ripple, Stellar, and stablecoin-based settlement networks reducing correspondent banking dependence.
competitorFactors.impact: Correspondent banking revenue erosion; FX margin compression; infrastructure replacement need

competitorFactors.name: RegTech Automation Competition
competitorFactors.description: Specialized regtech vendors (Chainalysis, ComplyAdvantage, Sumsub) offering superior AML/KYC automation vs incumbent platforms.
competitorFactors.impact: Incumbent platform replacement; specialist vendor best-of-breed preference; compliance cost reduction

competitorFactors.name: Cloud-Native Core Banking
competitorFactors.description: Mambu, Thought Machine, and Finxact offering cloud-native core platforms with 6-month implementation timelines.
competitorFactors.impact: Legacy core vendor displacement; speed-to-market advantage; cloud migration acceleration

competitorFactors.name: Robo-Advisor Scale Economics
competitorFactors.description: Betterment, Wealthfront, and Schwab Intelligent Portfolios driving automated advice to near-zero marginal cost.
competitorFactors.impact: Advisor productivity pressure; fee compression on digital advice; hybrid model necessity

competitorFactors.name: BNPL Mainstream Banking Adoption
competitorFactors.description: Affirm, Klarna, and Afterpay partnering with major banks while banks launch competing installment products.
competitorFactors.impact: Credit risk model disruption; partnership versus build decisions; regulatory scrutiny intensification

valueDomains.name: Fraud Loss Reduction
valueDomains.description: Reduction in financial losses from payment fraud, APP scams, identity theft, and synthetic fraud.

valueDomains.name: Loan Processing Efficiency
valueDomains.description: Reduction in cost-to-close, cycle time, and operational effort for mortgage, auto, and SMB lending.

valueDomains.name: Net Interest Margin Improvement
valueDomains.description: Expansion of NIM through deposit beta optimization, funding mix improvement, and pricing analytics.

valueDomains.name: AUM Growth
valueDomains.description: Increase in assets under management through advisor productivity, digital acquisition, and retention improvement.

valueDomains.name: Customer Lifetime Value
valueDomains.description: Improvement in CLV through retention, cross-sell, and experience enhancement reducing churn.

valueDomains.name: Churn Reduction
valueDomains.description: Reduction in customer attrition through predictive analytics, proactive service, and product stickiness.

valueDomains.name: Claims Leakage Reduction
valueDomains.description: Reduction in non-meritorious claim payments and loss adjustment expenses through fraud detection and process automation.

valueDomains.name: Underwriting Accuracy
valueDomains.description: Improvement in rate adequacy and risk selection through advanced analytics and alternative data.

valueDomains.name: Compliance Cost Reduction
valueDomains.description: Reduction in regulatory reporting, AML, and compliance operations cost through automation.

valueDomains.name: Audit Readiness
valueDomains.description: Improvement in control environment, documentation, and testing efficiency reducing audit cost and findings.

valueDomains.name: Risk-Adjusted Return Improvement
valueDomains.description: Enhancement of RAROC or economic profit through better risk measurement, pricing, and portfolio optimization.

valueDomains.name: Capital Efficiency (CET1 / RWA Optimization)
valueDomains.description: Improvement in capital ratios through RWA optimization, provision efficiency, and asset quality improvement.

governance.reviewOwner: Master Pack Foundation Architect - Financial Services
